body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Type:** Feature Request
**Component:** `Azure.Identity` — `ConfigurableCredential` / `ChainedTokenCredential`
**Assigned to:** @m-nash

This is a well-scoped feature request to add a programmatic hook for customizing `Transport` and `HttpPipelinePolicy` instances on credentials built from `IConfiguration`. The core gap is that `HttpPipelineTransport` is abstract and cannot be deserialized from config, and `AddPolicy()` is inherently code-only — so a code+config hybrid mechanism is needed.

<details>
<summary>📋 Summary of the Gap</summary>

| Area | Current Limitation |
|---|---|
| `Transport` | `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()` is always called; no config binding |
| `HttpPipelinePolicy` | `AddPolicy()` is programmatic-only, no config path |
| `ChainedTokenCredential` | Each credential in the chain has independent options; no parent propagation |
| DI integration | No first-class `IHttpClientFactory` integration in the credential options path |

</details>

<details>
<summary>💡 Design Ideas & Considerations</summary>

1. **Callback/delegate on options** — A `Action<TokenCredentialOptions>` (or `Action<TokenCredentialOptions, string credentialSourceName>`) callback registered on `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` / `ChainedTokenCredentialOptions` that fires before each credential is constructed. This is the most flexible and DI-friendly option.

2. **Post-config hook per source** — A dictionary-style API where callers register per-source customizers keyed by `CredentialSource` enum value. More granular but more verbose for "apply to all" scenarios.

3. **`IConfigureOptions<T>` integration (DI)** — Leverage ASP.NET Core's options pattern so that `IConfigureOptions<TokenCredentialOptions>` implementations registered in DI are automatically applied. Natural fit for the `IHttpClientFactory` use case.

4. **Propagation vs. per-credential** — For `ChainedTokenCredential`, decide whether the hook applies to all credentials uniformly (simpler) or per-credential-type (more control, but requires the callback to inspect the options type).

**Recommended starting point:** An `Action<TokenCredentialOptions>` delegate property on `ConfigurableCredentialOptions` / `ChainedTokenCredentialOptions` that receives each credential's options object before the credential is instantiated. This covers Transport injection, policy addition, and is easily testable.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Code Locations</summary>

- `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/src/` — main credential implementations
- `ClientOptions` constructor that accepts `IConfigurationSection` — where transport is hardcoded via `HttpPipelineTransport.Create()`
- `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` — where credential instances are created from `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` source elements
- `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` — options class that would need the new callback property

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks / Checklist</summary>

- [ ] **API design review** — Agree on the callback/delegate shape (`Action<TokenCredentialOptions>`, typed overloads, or DI-based)
- [ ] **Design for single credential path** — Hook into `ClientOptions(IConfigurationSection)` to call the delegate before transport creation
- [ ] **Design for `ChainedTokenCredential` path** — Propagate the delegate through `ChainedTokenCredentialFactory` to each credential's options
- [ ] **IHttpClientFactory scenario validation** — Write a prototype that injects a transport backed by `IHttpClientFactory` via the new hook to validate the design works end-to-end
- [ ] **Public API listing update** — Run `Export-API.ps1 identity` after implementing
- [ ] **Unit tests** — Cover single credential, chained credential, and DI integration scenarios
- [ ] **README / samples update** — Add a code sample showing the hybrid config+code customization pattern

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Useful Resources</summary>

- [Azure.Identity README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md)
- [HttpPipeline and transport customization docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#transport)
- [Azure SDK .NET Design Guidelines — pipeline policies](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-http-pipeline)
- [IHttpClientFactory with Azure SDK blog post](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/net-framework-compatibility/)

</details>
